label: Recent Trades
description: Live streaming feed of recent prediction market fills with market name, side, and profit.

l0.notes: All 9 checks green. 0.1: WidgetErrorBoundary confirmed in widget-wrapper.tsx. 0.2: requiredEntitlements=['predictions-trading']. 0.3: availableOn=['predictions']. 0.4: no any or @ts-ignore. 0.5: no lib/mocks/ imports in widget file. 0.6: synchronous mock context — no isLoading needed; LiveFeedWidget supports isLoading prop but context provides data synchronously; usePredictionsData() throws on missing provider, caught by WidgetErrorBoundary. 0.7: LiveFeedWidget receives isEmpty={fills.length===0} and emptyMessage='No recent fills yet' — empty state handled. 0.8: no error field in context; WidgetErrorBoundary (0.1) covers throw. 0.9: no dead code.

l1.notes: 1.1 PASS — recentFills read exclusively via usePredictionsData() hook. 1.2 PASS — context calls useGlobalScope(). 1.3-1.5 N/A — recentFills are per-trade events appended on placeTrade(); they are not filtered by org/client/strategy scope (fills belong to all strategies by design). 1.6 PASS — no scope filtering means all fills shown regardless of scope state. 1.7 KNOWN-GAP — MOCK_RECENT_FILLS lives in lib/mocks/fixtures/; future scope.

knownIssues.description: recentFills not filtered by org/client/strategy scope — fills are per-trade events that span all strategies by design. Scope filtering for fills would require a strategyId or clientId on each fill record (future schema work).
Reference data (MOCK_RECENT_FILLS) lives in lib/mocks/fixtures/ rather than lib/constants/; moving is future scope.
